[Sun 2026-04-05 15:16 UTC] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]: # Agent 4: Reports + Notifications Frontend

Make these 2 pages actually functional. Backend APIs are being added by Agent 1.

## Read First
- ~/magi-v2/frontend/reports/index.html
- ~/magi-v2/frontend/notifications/index.html
- ~/magi-v2/frontend/shared/core.js
- ~/magi-v2/frontend/shared/nav.js (check if notification badge exists)

## 1. Reports (~/magi-v2/frontend/reports/index.html)

Currently placeholder/empty. Make it show real generated reports.

**API endpoints (being created by Agent 1):**
- `GET /api/reports` → `{ ok, reports: [...] }`
- `POST /api/reports/generate` → generates a new report from real analytics data

Each report object:
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "title": "Weekly Report — 2026-04-05",
  "generatedAt": 1775380000000,
  "period": { "from": 1774775200000, "to": 1775380000000 },
  "data": {
    "sessions": 15,
    "totalTokens": 50000,
    "totalCost": "1.79",
    "tasksCompleted": 5,
    "tasksTotal": 12,
    "pipelineDeals": 3,
    "pipelineValue": 15000000,
    "topAgents": { "uriel": { "requests": 50 }, "rafael": { "requests": 30 } }
  }
}
```

**Page layout:**

**Header:** "📋 Reports" title + "Generate Report" button (primary cyan)

**Report List:**
- Cards, newest first, stagger-item animation
- Each card shows: title, generated date, key metrics preview (sessions, cost, tasks)
- Click card → expand to full report view

**Full Report View (expanded card or modal):**
- Period: "March 29 — April 5, 2026"
- Stats grid (4 cards): Sessions | Tokens Used | Total Cost | Tasks Done
- Agent Usage table: Agent name | Requests | (bar chart width proportional to usage)
- Pipeline summary: X deals, Rp X total value
- Use data-countup on numbers

**Empty state:** "No reports yet — click 'Generate Report' to create your first weekly report"

**Generate button:**
- Calls POST /api/reports/generate
- Shows loading spinner during generation
- On success, refreshes list and shows toast
- Disable button during generation

## 2. Notifications (~/magi-v2/frontend/notifications/index.html)

Currently always shows 0. Backend Agent 1 is adding real notification triggers.

**API endpoints (being created by Agent 1):**
- `GET /api/notifications` → `{ ok, items: [...], total, unread }`
- `PUT /api/notifications/read` → `{ id }` or `{ all: true }` to mark as read
- `DELETE /api/notifications` → clear all

Each notification:
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "category": "users",
  "title": "New registration",
  "body": "Kenny requested access",
  "read": false,
  "createdAt": 1775380000000
}
```

Categories: `users`, `pipeline`, `broadcast`, `reports`, `system`

**Page layout:**

**Header:** "🔔 Notifications" + unread badge count + "Mark All Read" button + "Clear All" button

**Notification List:**
- Cards, newest first, stagger-item
- Each card: category icon | title (bold if unread) | body | time ago
- Unread cards have a left cyan border (like Apex active state)
- Click card → marks as read

**Category icons:**
- users: 👥
- pipeline: 🔄
- broadcast: 📢
- reports: 📋
- system: ⚙️

**Empty state:** "🔔 No notifications — you're all caught up!"

**Auto-refresh every 30 seconds** to check for new notifications.

## 3. BONUS: Notification Badge in Sidebar Nav

Check ~/magi-v2/frontend/shared/nav.js — if possible, add a small red badge next to the 🔔 Notifications nav item showing unread count. This requires:
- Fetch GET /api/notifications on nav render
- If unread > 0, add a small badge element

If nav.js is too complex to modify safely, skip this — it's a bonus.

## Rules
- Read each file FULLY before editing
- Use shared api() function
- Use shared toast()
- Add loading/error/empty states
- Mobile responsive
- stagger-item on list items
- data-countup on stat numbers
- Format dates with timeAgo() from core.js
- Format currency as "Rp" + Indonesian number format
- Do NOT modify server.js
- Auto-refresh notifications every 30s (with cleanup on page unload)

return `<div class="rpt-card stagger-item" data-id="${r.id}">

Both use stagger-item in their template literals. Everything checks out. Let me also verify the nav.js change didn't break the export:

Good. Let me verify the end of nav.js to make sure the function closes properly:

Everything is clean and correct. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

---

## ✅ Completed: Reports + Notifications Frontend

### 1. Reports Page (`~/magi-v2/frontend/reports/index.html`) — Full Rewrite

**Before:** Showed raw analytics data (daily charts, agent performance tables from `/api/analytics` endpoints).

**After:** Shows generated report cards from `GET /api/reports` with:
- **Header:** "📋 Reports" + cyan "⚡ Generate Report" button
- **Report List:** Cards sorted newest-first with `stagger-item` animation, showing title, time ago, key metrics preview (sessions, cost, tasks)
- **Click to Expand:** Full report detail view with:
  - Period display ("March 29, 2026 — April 5, 2026")
  - Stats grid (4 cards): Sessions | Tokens Used | Total Cost | Tasks Done — all with `data-countup`
  - Agent Usage with proportional bar charts
  - Pipeline summary with "Rp" Indonesian format
- **Generate Button:** Calls `POST /api/reports/generate`, uses `setLoading()` spinner, disables during generation, shows toast on success, auto-refreshes list
- **Empty State:** "No reports yet — click 'Generate Report' to create your first weekly report"
- **Error State:** Handled with user-friendly message
- **Mobile responsive** (2-column stats grid on small screens)

### 2. Notifications Page (`~/magi-v2/frontend/notifications/index.html`) — Rewritten

**Before:** Used `data.notifications` shape and `n.type`/`n.source` fields.

**After:** Supports both `data.items` and `data.notifications` response shapes, uses category-based icons:
- **Header:** "🔔 Notifications" + unread badge (red when unread, green when zero) + "Mark All Read" + "Clear All"
- **Category Icons:** 👥 users | 🔄 pipeline | 📢 broadcast | 📋 reports | ⚙️ system
- **Notification Cards:** `stagger-item` animated, category icon | bold title if unread | body | time ago
- **Unread Style:** Left cyan border (matching Apex active state)
- **Click to Read:** Calls `PUT /api/notifications/read` with `{ id }` body
- **Mark All Read:** Calls `PUT /api/notifications/read` with `{ all: true }`
- **Clear All:** Confirmation dialog → `DELETE /api/notifications`
- **Auto-refresh:** Every 30 seconds, with `beforeunload` + `visibilitychange` cleanup
- **Empty state:** "🔔 No notifications — You're all caught up!"

### 3. BONUS: Notification Badge in Sidebar Nav (`~/magi-v2/frontend/shared/nav.js`)

Added `_updateNavNotifBadge()` function that:
- Fires on every `renderNav()` call (every page load)
- Fetches `GET /api/notifications` silently
- If unread > 0, injects a red badge (pill shape) next to the 🔔 Notifications nav item
- Shows "99+" for counts > 99
- Silent fail if API unavailable (bonus feature, shouldn't break nav)
- The notifications page also updates this badge dynamically on each load/refresh

### API Contract Used

| Endpoint | Method | Body |
|----------|--------|------|
| `/api/reports` | GET | — |
| `/api/reports/generate` | POST | — |
| `/api/notifications` | GET | — |
| `/api/notifications/read` | PUT | `{ id }` or `{ all: true }` |
| `/api/notifications` | DELETE | — |

**No modifications to server.js.** All using the shared `api()`, `toast()`, `escHtml()`, `timeAgo()`, `formatNumber()`, and `setLoading()` from core.js.
